content:
@everyone 这次公告图遗漏了重要的说明，是我们欠考虑了。引发了大家的担忧，我们深感抱歉。

新道具空幻之芯（Ethereal Core）的作用是：在版本期间内可以当作流明之芯（Luminous Core）使用，版本结束后会变为无垢之芯（Pure Core）。对应的所有发生变化的奖励，提供的银芯（Silver）奖励相较之前都有所提高。如果在版本内使用掉的话，总奖励比原先会有所提高。

本次版本在游戏系统机制、界面布局上会有较多习惯上的变化，习惯可能需要一些时间，这只是其中的改变之一。再考虑到产能不足导致的延期，这都给大家带来了不便，我们深感抱歉。

为了补偿大家，也为了向大家的耐心等待表达谢意，我们将会在版本发布后每天为大家发送 1000 银芯，持续 10 天！希望大家能一如既往在忘却前夜玩得愉快，我们脑缸组会始终保持聆听并为大家继续改进游戏！

This announcement image omitted important explanations — this was an oversight on our part, and we deeply apologize for the concern it has caused.

The new prop Ethereal Core (空幻之芯) works as follows: during the version period, it can be used as a Luminous Core (流明之芯), and after the version ends, it will transform into a Pure Core (无垢之芯). All corresponding rewards that have been changed now provide increased Silver (银芯) rewards compared to before. If you use them up during the version, the total rewards will be higher than before.

This version brings quite a few changes to the game’s system mechanics and interface layout, and it may take some time to get used to — this is just one of those changes. Combined with the delay caused by insufficient production capacity, all of this has caused inconvenience, and we sincerely apologize.

To compensate everyone and to express our gratitude for your patient wait, we will be sending 1,000 Silver per day for 10 days after the version launches! We hope everyone continues to enjoy Morimens as always, and we at the B.I.A.V. Studio will keep listening and continue improving the game for you!​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

content:
I understand this is a gacha and business is business, but...
I really don't see what the hope behind this change is. This punishes f2ps the most, and this gacha honestly is kinda hard to recommend to a lot of f2p friends right now. I love this game, the style, the gameplay. I advocated to join the translation team if needed ages ago. I can't recommend it to any of my friends though, and this is part of why. The heavy dupe reliance, reducing auto play options, adding +12 bonuses (potentially to limiteds) when they could be more accessible buffs, and now this? It hurts, and is really confusing. 

I don't mean this to be mean, but I don't feel like B.I.A.V. can afford to make anti-player decisions like this, not quietly like this. We need communication, ideally before these changes are made. And frankly, these kinds of monetization practices hurt the people that have faith in this game's vision and would otherwise spread the word.

content:
Thank you for your attention and feedback — it genuinely means a lot.

You’re right that this change should have come with a proper devlog. I’m sorry it didn’t. Going forward, we’ll do our best to communicate major changes like this before they go live, not after — that’s a fair ask, and we hear you.

I also want to apologize for the silence — after 2.4, the team went through significant challenges and internal changes. That said, we’re more hopeful now than we’ve been in a long time: a writer who contributed many of the major story arcs in our 1.0 era is about to rejoin the team.

I won’t pretend the recent changes don’t add pressure. But we genuinely believe they’re necessary for the game’s long-term health. Let me address your concerns one by one:

On dupe reliance: You’re right, this is a real problem. Some of it stems from decisions made at launch that we can’t fully undo. What we have done over the past two years is lower the hard pity count and increase rewards several times over compared to launch — trying to ease the burden as much as we can.

On auto-battle: When we launched the JP version, we spent a lot of time debating where auto should and shouldn’t apply. Originally, we weren’t going to include it at all — that actually matched what the community was asking for back then, with concerns that the game would lose its depth and “thinking” element if auto became universal. Unfortunately, due to some bugs, auto-battle ended up with even wider coverage on the international server than on JP. Our intent is for auto to work on all repetitive, lower-challenge content, but to not offer it for the bi-weekly highest-challenge content.
thread_title: 📜 V2.5.0 版本前瞻·上┊Version Outlook - Part Ⅰ

content:
On +12 bonuses: We’ve discussed this extensively and have communicated about it before. Our core principle is that the game will never require +12 to play — these are bonus rewards for +12, and existing content won’t have its difficulty raised because of them.

On the new reward policy: The goal of this change is to make in-game resource circulation more dynamic, while raising the overall reward level for any given version. After careful evaluation, we found this new approach delivers a better experience in terms of fun and timely feedback. We fully understand that this does shift habits for players who prefer to stockpile resources long-term, and we don’t take that lightly.

And thank you for your willingness to help with translation back then — that kind of support from players like you is exactly why we want to do better.

content:
Thank you, and I do hope that can be the case. It's been tough for the studio since the start and I can definitely understand that, and I do hope having a previous writer rejoin can help there. 

I also know changing monetization to fill a studio's needs is important at well. I realize what I'm about to ask may not be feasible, but would it be worth running votes on changes again? Maybe even and in-game survey through the mail? Just saying... "hey, this is where we're at, and unfortunately changes need to be made. Of these options, what sounds the most accommodating? If none of these, can you leave your own suggestions?"
It's a big ask, again. Maybe a smaller version of that, but I feel it could lessen the anxiety from moments like this. 

As for the other changes:

The auto-battle one was made while I was away due to family related reasons, so that's on me for being hasty with my judgment to a point. I'm a big Limbus player and I do agree win rate/auto play like that can take away from learning the game, I always suggest people to not use them if possible, but it is still a QoL feature being taken away. 

The dupes and such: That makes a lot of sense, and honestly I wish I had suggestions or something, but I know it isn't my place and these are decisions that were made very early on. The +12 AA bonuses are more "I really hope these don't become a problem" more than "they are a problem right now". My partner bounced off the game with one of her stated reason being she didn't like the OE mechanic (feeling like she *had* to pull 8 total copies), so I worry if other new players will be intimidated by seeing AA as a feature. 

I also want to add a quick aside here as a thank you, for your reply. I know you're probably a bit swamped at the moment, and you get plenty of messages and replies from the community. It may be worth making a larger statement when the dust settles, but I'm thankful for what you do for the community Light. I hope for the best for both the team and the game itself.

content:
Thank you — really. The community is bigger now than it’s ever been, and it’s been steadily growing in a healthier direction. You’re right that we should slow down and communicate more openly before major system changes, not after. Whatever form that takes — in-game polls, mail surveys, community votes — I’ll genuinely think about your suggestion and see what we can do.

I want to be upfront about V2.5: some of the adjustments we’re making are going to feel like invasive surgery. They’re necessary, but I won’t pretend they’ll be painless. For V2.5.1, we’ll be listening closely and making further fixes and quality-of-life improvements based on what we hear from everyone.

On OE and the AA bonuses specifically — I hear your concern, especially the example about your partner. The worry that new players will look at OE/AA and feel “I have to pull 8 copies” is exactly the kind of perception we don’t want to set in. I can’t promise a fix overnight, but it’s something we’ll keep watching carefully, and your feedback here genuinely helps frame how we think about it.

And thank you for the kind words at the end — they mean more than you might think. We’ll keep doing our best for the game, and for the people who care about it like you do.

content:
Honestly, auto-battle was something we agonized over for a long time. We do want every piece of content to feel meaningfully challenging — and frankly, I think we push harder on that than most games in our genre do. But even with that, I have to be honest: players come in with different build levels, different team compositions, different goals. There’s no realistic way to guarantee the game feels engaging at every single moment.

Given that, we came to the view that making the boring stretches lighter — letting auto handle the parts that aren’t really testing anything — actually has value. It’s not about lowering the bar for the game as a whole; it’s about respecting players’ time when the content genuinely isn’t asking anything of them.

So no, there’s no “keeping parity with JP to link the games” angle driving this. The reasoning stands on its own.

content:
Thanks for the questions! Let me address them:

On Ethereal Cores replacing Luminous Cores: Yes, that’s the current plan — starting with the Arachne event, Ethereal Cores will generally replace Luminous Cores in event shops/exchanges. However, some special events will still use Luminous Cores, such as the upcoming School Active event.

As for the Ethereal Cores themselves: they won’t disappear, but they will transform after the version ends. During the version, you can use them as Luminous Cores; any that remain when the version ends will automatically convert into Pure Cores and stay in your inventory. More details are in our follow-up announcement.

On 1★ skins and the free period: You’re right that most 1★ skins have been given out through events first, and that’s been our general approach. To be transparent: nearly all 1★ skins are variant illustrations or minor variations of previously released art — that’s been our expectation for 1★ skins from the start.

That said, this won’t necessarily apply to every skin going forward, and there may be special arrangements in certain cases. For Helot and Nautila specifically, the skins are still event rewards — you don’t need to spend any Silver Prime you already have. Earning Silver Prime through the event is simply part of the process.
